title: Outgoing user-notification / messaging backbone
description: Datum has no outbound messaging surface to communicate with users. Design: typed Notice record in gui-protocol (id/source/severity/category/title/body/actions/condition/timestamp), presented by the shell as three tiers — transient toasts (bottom-right, auto-expire), sticky condition-banners (KiCad-infobar style, auto-clear when condition resolves), and a persistent Notices log behind a status-bar bell. Publishers state facts; shell decides presentation. Never journaled (consumer/env state, not a design mutation). Headless surfaces render same notices as text/JSON. Optional chime on warning+ severity, globally disableable, redundant with icon/color/text (accessibility). Shared-by-construction backbone, not a one-off dialog. Weave into UI_LAYOUT_SYSTEM_CONTRACT or a decision record + PROGRESS/Frontier.

title: DWT: notify user KDE 'disable touchpad while typing' blocks Space-drag pan
description: KDE Wayland default 'Disable while typing' suppresses touchpad motion whenever a key (incl. held Space) is down, blocking the Space+left-drag pan gesture. First client of the notification backbone: condition-bound sticky banner. Startup probe parses ~/.config/kcminputrc [Libinput][vid][pid][dev] groups — key DisableWhileTyping is ABSENT at the blocking default (present only once user unchecks it), so absence=trap-live is a deterministic signal. Escalate severity if no external mouse is enumerated (touchpad is the only pan device). Action button launches 'systemsettings kcm_touchpad'. Banner auto-clears when probe sees the fix. GNOME sibling adapter later (gsettings org.gnome.desktop.peripherals.touchpad disable-while-typing).

title: Terminal input-focus authority: workspace hotkeys leak into PTY; cannot type into terminal
description: Root cause: the gui-app keyboard router (crates/gui-app/src/main.rs ~L503-896) has no focus authority — it gates every arm on ui.active_dock_tab (is the dock OPEN) as a proxy for focus, and never consults pane focus (decision 021 / status-bar 'focus Board'). Symptoms: (1) with terminal dock open+attached, terminal_accepts_raw_input()=true so the raw-PTY arm swallows all keys — 'f' (board fit) is written to the shell (activity span in:1B out:1B | F), workspace tool hotkeys s/b/v/m/x are guarded on active_dock_tab.is_none() so they can't fire; screenshot shows leaked DDFFFFFFFF at the prompt. (2) Deliberate typing lands in a hidden legacy line-editor buffer when detached. Fix: one keyboard-focus owner (Editor(pane)|Terminal|Overlay); opening dock never steals keys; click/Ctrl-focus to enter terminal, Esc/canvas to leave; rewire the \x1b[I/\x1b[O focus sequences from WINDOW focus (main.rs:328) to TERMINAL focus; visible focus (block vs hollow cursor). Also: hotkeys fire on Release while PTY consumes on Press — move hotkeys to Press once routing is fixed. Blocks trustworthy terminal-as-messaging-destination.

title: Terminal has two rival input models (raw PTY vs legacy line-editor)
description: The dock terminal has two input paths selected implicitly by attach state: raw PTY byte writer, and a legacy structured line-editor (append_dock_text -> ui.terminal.input with its own cursor/history/tab-complete, main.rs ~L559-790). Detached typing silently lands in the hidden line buffer instead of the shell. Collapse to one: attached PTY gets raw bytes; detached shows a read-only screen with an explicit reattach affordance. The line-editor is a pre-PTY relic (two-rival-implementations defect class). Same subsystem as terminal-focus-authority.

title: App-owned Output dock tab for stdout / diagnostics (distinct from PTY)
description: Second lane of the messaging problem: piping stdout/engine/daemon/GUI diagnostics to the user. Do NOT inject app messages into the shell terminal screen — it hosts a FOREIGN shell and injection corrupts the prompt (exactly what Codex's live pan-gesture trace spam is doing now: ACTIVITY SPANS / 'selected terminal activity span' lines burying the prompt). Unlike Claude Code's TUI which owns its whole screen, Datum's terminal hosts an external shell. Field answer (VS Code): two dock tabs — Terminal (pure PTY, untouchable) + Output (app-owned, read-only, streamed, source-filterable). Dock is already multi-tab. Becomes archival home for the Notice log and the correct destination for Codex's gesture traces (which should leave the shell screen regardless).

title: Migrate stray in-repo tracker files into beads
description: Two ad-hoc issue/backlog files predate this tracker and should fold in once beads is adopted: docs/gui/M7_IMPORTED_BOARD_FIDELITY_ISSUES.md (184 lines) and docs/DECOMPOSITION_BACKLOG.md (241 lines). Not bulk-migrated during the trial — item-by-item classification is owner/Codex judgment. Evidence that an unmanaged tracker need was already materializing in the repo.
